# Savan Padaliya

> Full Stack AI Engineer specializing in AI integrations, scalable Node.js backend systems, and SaaS development. Available for freelance, consulting, and full-time opportunities.

Savan Padaliya is a Full Stack AI Engineer with 4+ years of experience building intelligent web applications, AI automation pipelines, and production-grade SaaS products. He integrates OpenAI, Gemini, Vertex AI, LangChain, and N8N into real-world products — from WhatsApp AI chatbots to SEO/AEO/GEO optimization bots.

## About

- Full name: Savan Padaliya
- Role: Full Stack AI Engineer
- Experience: 4+ years in JavaScript, Node.js, and AI integrations
- Community: GDG (Google Developer Group) Organiser
- Website: https://www.savanpadaliya.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/savanpadaliya/
- Twitter: https://twitter.com/padaliya_savan

## Services

- [Web & Backend Development](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/): Scalable Node.js APIs, microservices, and full-stack web applications built for production
- [AI Integrations](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/): Chatbots, automation pipelines, and LLM-powered features using OpenAI, Gemini, and LangChain
- [SaaS Development](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/): End-to-end SaaS product development with AI capabilities baked in
- [Technical Consulting](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/about/): Expert guidance on software architecture, AI adoption, and technology strategy

## Book a Session

- [1:1 Mentoring & Consulting](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/topmate): Book a 1:1 session — career guidance, AWS infrastructure, or project scoping. Free sessions available.

## Tech Stack

- Languages & Frameworks: JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, React, Angular, Next.js, Express.js
- AI & Automation: OpenAI API, Gemini API, Vertex AI, LangChain, N8N, Google ADK, Google MCP
- Databases: MongoDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Redis
- Cloud: AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, DigitalOcean
- DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes, Nginx, Apache Kafka

## Blog Posts

- [RAG Architecture for JavaScript Developers](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/rag-architecture-for-javascript-developers/): A practical guide to building Retrieval Augmented Generation pipelines using JavaScript, LangChain.js, and OpenAI — with working Node.js code examples.
- [Vertex AI Setup for Node.js Apps](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/vertex-ai-setup-for-nodejs-apps/): Step-by-step guide to integrating Google Vertex AI into Node.js — from project setup and authentication to making your first generative AI call.
- [OpenAI vs Vertex AI for Production SaaS](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/openai-vs-vertex-ai-for-production-saas/): Practical comparison of OpenAI and Vertex AI for teams building production SaaS — covering cost, reliability, model quality, latency, and vendor lock-in.
- [How to Monitor AI Pipelines in Production](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/how-to-monitor-ai-pipelines-in-production/): How to monitor LLM-powered applications — tracking latency, token usage, error rates, and hallucinations using practical tools and strategies.
- [Setting Up AI Workloads on Google Cloud](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/google-cloud-ai-setup-guide/): Beginner's guide to setting up AI workloads on Google Cloud — AutoML, Cloud Functions, Cloud Run, and Vertex AI.
- [JavaScript vs TypeScript: The Dilemma](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/type-or-not-to-type-the-javascript-vs-typescript-dilemma/): Comprehensive comparison of JavaScript and TypeScript — advantages, disadvantages, and when to use each.
- [LangChain.js Agents: Build Your First AI Agent in Node.js](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/langchainjs-agents-nodejs-tutorial/): A practical guide to building autonomous AI agents with LangChain.js in Node.js — tools, agent executors, memory, and real working code examples.
- [Choosing a Vector Database: Chroma vs Pinecone vs pgvector](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/vector-database-comparison-chroma-pinecone-pgvector/): A practical comparison of Chroma, Pinecone, and pgvector for production RAG pipelines — covering setup, query performance, scalability, and cost.
- [Prompt Engineering for Production LLM Apps](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/prompt-engineering-production-llm-apps/): A practical guide to prompt engineering for production LLM applications — system prompts, temperature, context injection, few-shot examples, and output validation.
- [Deploying AI Apps to Cloud Run: Node.js + Vertex AI](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/deploy-nodejs-ai-app-cloud-run/): Step-by-step guide to deploying a Node.js + Vertex AI application to Google Cloud Run — Dockerfile, authentication, Artifact Registry, and continuous deployment.
- [How to Reduce LLM API Costs in Production](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/reduce-llm-api-costs-production/): Practical strategies to cut OpenAI and Gemini API costs in production — model selection, caching, prompt optimization, batching, and RAG tuning.
- [Password Hashing with Bcrypt in Node.js](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/password-hashing-bcrypt-nodejs/): Learn why bcrypt is the right choice for password hashing in Node.js, how bcryptjs works under the hood, and the mistakes that leave your users exposed.
- [TypeScript for AI Applications: Typing LLM Responses in Node.js](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/typescript-for-ai-applications-nodejs/): How to properly type OpenAI and Vertex AI SDK responses in TypeScript — typed prompts, structured outputs, Zod validation, and patterns that prevent runtime surprises.
- [Password Security Best Practices for Web Developers](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/password-security-best-practices-web-developers/): OWASP-aligned password security — hashing, storage, reset flows, timing attacks, credential stuffing defence, and the mistakes that leave users vulnerable.
- [Why Most AI Features Fail](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/why-ai-features-fail-product-thinking/): Most AI features fail not because of the model but because of poor problem definition. What product-minded engineers do differently.
- [System Design for AI-Powered SaaS](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/system-design-ai-powered-saas/): How to architect AI-powered SaaS at scale — async queues, provider fallbacks, caching, cost control, and observability for LLM-backed systems.
- [Cryptographic Randomness in JavaScript](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/cryptographic-randomness-javascript/): Math.random() is not secure for tokens or passwords. How to use crypto.getRandomValues() and Node.js crypto for cryptographically secure randomness.
- [Node.js TypeScript REST API Guide](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/nodejs-typescript-rest-api-production/): Production REST API patterns with TypeScript and Express — typed routes, Zod validation, error middleware, auth middleware, and typed environment variables.
- [UUID v4 in JavaScript: When to Use One](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/uuid-explained-when-to-use-javascript/): UUID v4 vs v7, GUID vs UUID, crypto.randomUUID(), and when to use UUIDs over auto-increment IDs for database primary keys and API entity identifiers.
- [How to Add AI to Your Existing Product Without Rebuilding It](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/how-to-add-ai-to-existing-product/): Add AI to your existing product without a rewrite — integration points, build vs. buy decisions, and the three patterns that cover most use cases.
- [What a 5-Day AI MVP Actually Looks Like](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/what-ai-mvp-looks-like/): Exactly what gets built in a 5-day AI MVP sprint — features, scope, stack, and realistic cost expectations for founders validating a product idea.
- [AI Engineer vs. Agency vs. In-House: Which Fits Your Stage?](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/hire-ai-engineer-vs-agency-vs-build-in-house/): When to hire a freelance AI engineer, use an agency, or build in-house — with cost comparison and the common mistakes at each stage.
- [Developers Are Great at HOW. But What About WHY and WHAT?](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/developers-missing-why-and-what/): Most developers are experts at HOW to build — but WHY and WHAT to build are where real product thinking lives. A Design Sprint changed how I see this gap.
- [Junior Developers: The Job Market Isn't Your Enemy — Mediocrity Is](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/junior-developer-job-market-fear/): Companies hiring 10 interns and keeping 2 isn't a market collapse — it's a filter. Here's what junior developers must do to be in the 2 that stay and build careers worth having.
- [What Actually Happens When You Hit Enter After Typing a URL](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/blogs/what-happens-when-you-type-a-url/): Most devs give the 3-step answer. Here is the full version — DNS, TCP, TLS, load balancers, app servers, databases — with the depth that actually gets you hired.

## Free Developer Tools

14 free, client-side developer tools — no sign-up required, everything runs in the browser.

- [JSON Formatter & Validator](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/json-formatter): Format, validate, or minify JSON with syntax highlighting.
- [JSON ↔ CSV Converter](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/json-csv-converter): Convert between JSON arrays and CSV format instantly.
- [Markdown Previewer](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/markdown-previewer): Live side-by-side editor and rendered preview for GitHub Flavored Markdown.
- [Diff Checker](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/diff-checker): Paste two texts and see added, removed, and unchanged lines highlighted.
- [Case Converter](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/case-converter): Convert text to camelCase, snake_case, PascalCase, kebab-case, and more.
- [Base64 Encoder / Decoder](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/base64): Encode plain text to Base64 or decode Base64 back to text. Unicode supported.
- [Regex Tester](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/regex-tester): Test regular expressions with live match highlighting and a built-in cheat sheet.
- [QR Code Generator](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/qr-generator): Generate a QR code from any URL or text. Download as PNG.
- [Random String Generator](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/random-string): Cryptographically random strings for tokens, API keys, and secrets.
- [Password Generator](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/password-generator): Generate strong passwords with a built-in strength meter.
- [UUID / GUID Generator](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/uuid-generator): Generate one or many UUID v4 identifiers. Copy all in one click.
- [Lorem Ipsum Generator](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/lorem-ipsum): Generate placeholder paragraphs, sentences, or word lists.
- [Bcrypt Hash Generator](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/bcrypt-generator): Hash passwords with bcrypt or verify a password against a hash.
- [Cron Job Builder](https://www.savanpadaliya.com/tools/cron-builder): Build cron expressions visually with a human-readable description.
